content_gaps: The SERPs are dominated by generic gift guides and direct-to-product pages. There's a significant lack of editorial content that explores the deeper emotional resonance of retirement as a life transition, rather than just a party occasion.

Commonly cited symbols (tree of life, compass, butterfly) are presented without much depth. There is an opportunity to explore these and other, less common, symbols with more nuance and sincerity, connecting them to the specific feelings of closing a major life chapter.

The language is often cliché-ridden, using phrases like 'celebrate a well-earned milestone,' 'honor her years of hard work,' and 'perfect way to say congratulations.' A more grounded, quiet voice would stand out.

There is very little content that speaks to the retiree themselves, acknowledging the complex mix of emotions—pride, loss, anticipation, uncertainty—that can accompany this transition. The focus is almost entirely on the gift-giver.
